108.status: Exited in seventh inning on May 23 after initially injuring ankle on April 4. Tested it on May 26, but felt worse the next day and underwent an MRI exam that revealed ankle tendinitis on May 28.
Same knee that sidelined Moncada last season. Underwent MRI exam and won't need surgery, but is continuing to get treatment without starting baseball activities.

Made four rehab appearances before being shut down. MRI came back clean May 15. Eligible to return from the 60-day IL May 26. Rehabbing at the club's complex in Arizona and resumed his throwing program.
Left April 29 outing before the start of the third inning. Targeting to start throwing program in late May/early June with a return date shortly after the All-Star break.

Exited in the fifth inning on May 6 after injuring himself while trying to beat out an infield single. Said he felt a pop and has started to put light pressure on his foot but is still a long way from returning.
Was evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister in Texas and underwent season-ending ligament and flexor tendon repair surgery, the club announced April 8.
Not going to play in 2026 and had the last year of his contract deferred over several years. Was placed on 60-day IL on March 24.

110.status: Exited May 27 game vs. the Rays in the eighth inning after showing visible discomfort. Underwent imaging. Threw in bullpen on May 29 and trending in right direction to avoid IL stint.
Injury was sustained in days after April 18 start in Cleveland. Joined team in St. Petersburg, Fla., for series vs. Rays on May 18. Transferred to 60-day IL on May 29.
Exited May 7 start in Miami due to left forearm discomfort. Was examined by Dr. Keith Meister and received cortisone shot on May 19.
Was evaluated on May 19 due to mild shoulder symptoms. Continuing to discuss treatment options. No clear timeline for return.
Imaging came back clean. Took anti-inflammatory medication. Began treatment May 4. Threw for first time on May 15 at Nationals Park in Washington, then again May 16.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery on May 13. Expected to recover in time to return during the early part of the 2027 season.
Scratched from lineup ahead of May 12 game vs. the Yankees after feeling discomfort during pregame work. Underwent an MRI and was placed on IL the next day.
Got second opinion with Dr. Neal ElAttrache in Los Angeles on April 20. Has inflammation in shoulder joint. Felt soreness coming out of March 31 outing.
Exited April 11 game vs. Giants after stumbling between first and second and sliding awkwardly into the bag on a double. Broke fourth metatarsal on left foot. Expected to miss substantial time.
Exited 2026 debut on March 31 due to right elbow discomfort. After getting imaging and a second opinion, underwent Tommy John surgery on April 8.
Underwent surgery to repair torn rotator cuff and torn labrum last Aug. 19. Threw for first time March 3, tossing 25 pitches from 45 feet on flat ground.

111.status: Threw off flat ground on May 28 after getting pain-killing injection, but will be down for three to four days after experiencing discomfort. Club believes stay on the IL should be for the minimum.
Threw an inning of live batting practice on May 26, marking the first time he faced hitters since going on the injured list. Crochet will throw multiple innings against hitters on June 2.
Threw another side session on May 27; could throw live batting practice by the weekend.
Could do some light swinging toward the end of the the week, interim manager Chad Tracy said on May 27.
Underwent surgery in Philadelphia from Dr. William Meyers. Per Red Sox interim manager Chad Tracy, he will be "out a while."
Continues to throw off flat ground.
Will soon start forearm strengthening exercises with an eye toward throwing by mid-June.

111.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament right elbow/right flexor pronator strain
111.status: Making 75 throws out to 105 feet three days a week, and 30 throws at 60 feet three days a week while throwing changeups those days. Targeting mound work in late June.

112.status: Began throwing seamless plyometric balls on May 26. Will throw an extended bullpen session on May 31.
Expected to start a rehab assignment with Triple-A Iowa on June 2.
Left knee meniscectomy (May 7). Simulated multiple innings off mound on May 22 and 26. Expected to make two Minor League rehab starts, the first on May 31 with Triple-A Iowa.
Cleared to begin strengthening exercises on May 26.
Felt some discomfort in April 23 outing. Further testing showed a flexor strain; timetable estimated to be around eight weeks. Transferred to 60-day IL on May 17.
Had setback (stress reaction near triceps/shoulder) after May 7 bullpen session, per manager Craig Counsell. Shut down for a month and moved rehab to Arizona. Transferred to 60-day IL on May 17.

112.playerName: Jaxon Wiggins (Cubs' No. 3 prospect, No. 88 overall)
112.status: Last pitched on April 4 for Iowa. Moved to Minor League IL out of caution. Has resumed playing catch, per president of baseball operations Jed Hoyer.
Underwent reconstruction of ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace April 20 via Dr. Keith Meister. Initial timetable for return is 12-14 months.
Underwent a right UCL revision repair with an internal brace on April 16. Timetable for return is estimated around 15-16 months.
Underwent a right patellar tendon debridement on Feb. 24. Could miss months while recovering.
Underwent surgery to repair a torn right UCL and flexor tendon last October. Signed a two-year deal with Chicago on Feb. 16 with an eye toward contributing in 2027.

113.status: Was diagnosed with a sprain on May 29 but will get a second opinion. Was prescribed a PRP injection, which would happen on June 2 if the first diagnosis is confirmed.
Back in Cincinnati on May 29 to get examined. If doctors approve, would be cleared to begin a throwing program.
Threw a second bullpen session on May 29, with a third slated for June 1. Scheduled to be around the team in Cincinnati for the homestand before returning to Goodyear, Ariz., to continue his rehab.
Faced hitters in live BP on May 27 and threw 45 pitches over three innings. Club is targeting a June 2 rehab assignment start for Triple-A Louisville at Memphis.
Scheduled to get a second MRI on May 29 to see how much activity he should be allowed to do by the medical staff.
A second MRI exam to check on his healing is scheduled for May 29.
Will head to Arizona to rehab.

114.status: Doing baseball activities and hitting in cage. Initial timeline was four to eight weeks. Transferred to 60-day IL on May 29.
MRI results confirmed inflammation but revealed no structural damage. No precise timeline yet for return.

115.status: Lined up to make his return against the Giants on May 30 after having thrown five innings in a rehab start with Triple-A Albuquerque on May 24.
Limited to rest and recovery for an injury he sustained on May 12 and tried to play through until undergoing an MRI just before being placed on the IL.
On a regimen of rest and treatment. He and the club decided against attempting to accelerate healing with a PRP injection.
Doing treatment and some pool exercises after days of extreme discomfort.
Making steady progress in his overall rehabilitation program. Participated in fielding drills on May 29.

115.playerName: Ethan Holliday (Rockies' No. 1 prospect, No. 17 overall)
115.status: After receiving the results of multiple examinations, will undergo season-ending surgery, a source told MLB.com. Recovery period will last until late in the year.
Underwent MRI after being put on the IL. Not likely to require surgery, per MLB source.
Played catch on May 24; noted that he felt good. Showing consistent progress in his shoulder rehabilitation program and was scheduled to play catch again May 25.

115.expectedReturn: To be determined after he is cleared for game action
115.status: Continued throwing program and playing catch; officially scheduled to throw a live bullpen session at the end of the month.
Did not travel to Los Angeles; remained in Denver where he is continuing to progress in his running rehab program.

Underwent ligament transfer Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on March 4.
No return in sight for Bryant, who has had a long and painful time since signing with the Rockies before the 2022 season.

Threw a bullpen session on May 29 at Rate Field and will throw a sim game on June 1 at Tropicana Field.
Completed a full workout on May 29. Team will determine next steps later in the weekend.
Mize will require a “slower week,” per manager A.J. Hinch, so team decided to place him on IL, but could be a minimum IL stint.
Saw a specialist on May 29, will rejoin team at Tampa Bay to continue his rehab with Major-League staff.
Scheduled to throw a bullpen session on May 30 at Rate Field.
Transferred to 60-day IL on May 29. Saw a specialist and is shut down from throwing.

Tossed 4 1/3 innings of one-run ball with three walks and three strikeouts in rehab start for Triple-A Toledo on May 25. Will pitch another game on May 31, either returning from IL to pitch for Tigers or remaining with Toledo.
Threw latest bullpen session on May 26.

Had a cortisone injection after he reported shoulder soreness following a relief outing in which he allowed one run in two-thirds of an inning on May 21. Was placed on the 15-day IL before Detroit’s May 22 game in Baltimore.

117.status: Went 0-for-4 as the designated hitter in his second Minor League rehab game with Triple-A Sugar Land on May 28 after going 1-for-2 and playing seven innings in left field the night before.

Threw live batting practice on May 29, his first time facing hitters since he suffered his injury. Touched 91-92 mph during the live BP, manager Joe Espada said.
Has begun a throwing progression. Was scratched before his scheduled start against the Twins in Minneapolis on May 19.
Has started a throwing progression. He first experienced soreness during his throwing program on May 8 and was sent home to Houston to see doctors.

Scheduled to make second Minor League rehab start, this one at Triple-A Sugar Land on May 29. Threw two scoreless innings May 24 for Double-A Corpus Christi.
Has begun running and swinging, building toward hitting off a tee next.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in left elbow
117.status: Underwent Tommy John surgery on Sept. 23, 2025.

118.status: Was not recovering well after throwing for past week. Had testing done May 29, which will determine severity and next steps.

Suffered shoulder injury while rehabbing foot injury May 6. Began playing catch again on May 27.
Threw a live BP on May 27 at Kauffman Stadium and looked great, according to manager Matt Quatraro. Will throw bullpen May 29 and is on track to come off IL when eligible on May 31.
Has begun his throwing progression, but still dealing with general arm fatigue. Continuing to play catch.

Underwent another elbow surgery May 22 to try to help continued problems with soreness and inflammation. Return-to-play timeline is unknown so far and will depend on how McArthur recovers in the short-term.
Underwent left labral repair surgery on April 28, ending his season.
After missing 2025 with shoulder issues, underwent right labral surgery in October. Rehabbing in Surprise, Ariz., during Spring Training but likely out for year.

Underwent the NanoNeedle Scope procedure to remove loose bodies from his elbow on May 19, which should allow for a faster return.
Faced hitters in live batting practice on May 29, which may be his last box to check before being activated once his 15 days are up.
Faced hitters for the third time on May 26 at Dodger Stadium. After two more live sessions, Phillips could begin a rehab assignment.
Faced hitters for the first time on May 26 but continued to feel some discomfort in his foot.

Set to begin a throwing progression during the week of May 25.

Rehabbing at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.

121.status: Began a rehab assignment May 27 with Double-A Binghamton and could return to the big club the first week of June. Will likely need to manage pain all season.

Originally expected to miss six to eight weeks but is progressing much more rapidly than expected. Took full batting practice on the field on May 29 and could begin a Minor League rehab assignment the week of June 1.
Made rehab starts on May 22 and 28, allowing four earned runs over seven combined innings. Will need to show better results before the Mets will consider activating him.
Timeline will remain fluid until symptoms subside. Was progressing better as of a May 29 update and could advance to baseball activities in the relatively near future.
Is expected to miss 2-3 weeks with a flexor strain suffered while running the bases in a May 25 game. Timetable will become more specific once he restarts baseball activities.
Has started baseball activities -- fielding drills, running sprints and swinging the bat in the indoor cages. It is not known when he will begin a rehab assignment.

Fractured his leg on a comebacker May 15. Will need 6-8 weeks to heal and another six or so for a Spring Training-style build-up. That should take him well past the All-Star break in a best-case scenario.
Fractured his thumb sliding into first base in a May 2 loss to the Angels. Will miss 6-8 weeks.
Fractured a rib during a March 5 game against the Nationals. Will need 4-8 weeks to recover, after which he can commence his buildup.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2027.

133.status: MRI on May 25 revealed "moderate" tendinitis in his rotator cuff. A's will provide more information on a potential timeline for his return at a later date.
Began a hitting progression May 25 by hitting off a tee. Is playing catch and will start ground-ball work the week of May 25. No timetable for return.
Took batting practice and performed infield drills on May 25 at Sutter Health Park. No timetable for return.
Pulled from rehab assignment after sustaining a "high-grade" hamstring strain on May 19. Will receive a platelet-rich plasma injection May 26 and will be reassessed after the All-Star break.
Shut down from throwing for a minimum of three weeks, beginning May 18. Received an ultrasound guided cortisone injection on May 19.
Underwent season-ending left hip surgery on May 19. Will rehab the rest of this season in hopes of being ready for 2027 Spring Training.

134.status: Suffered apparent injury on May 9 in San Francisco. Foot began swelling during flight to Pittsburgh on May 10. Cleared by doctor on May 29 to resume weight lifting activities in shoes. Will be reassessed during June 1-4 trip to Houston.
Resumed baseball activities on May 13; Rehabbing at team's training facilities in Bradenton, Fla. Playing catch on flat ground and is anticipated to throw a bullpen session at start of June.

135.status: Landed on the IL after May 1 start. Márquez threw a live batting practice session May 28. He’s expected to next make a Minor League rehab appearance, location TBD.
Fouled a ball off his toe on May 5, sustaining what the team said was a small fracture. Making notable progress -- is swinging pain-free and catching bullpens.
Underwent surgery in October 2024. Was hopeful for an early-season return but experienced a setback after making one start in Spring Training. Could be nearing the start of a throwing progression.
Expected to pitch again this season, after MRI revealed a flexor strain. Could be nearing the start of a throwing progression.
Placed on the injured list one day after working two scoreless innings in relief at Milwaukee. Began a throwing program on May 23. Could return as a reliever.
Underwent UCL internal brace surgery in April 2025. Began a rehab stint on May 7. Had his rehab transferred to Double-A San Antonio on May 17. Is building up as a starter.
Experienced concussion symptoms after a fastball off the chin on April 18. Initially cleared protocols, but symptoms arose in the days afterward. Has not resumed baseball activity.
Underwent surgery to repair the flexor tendon in his right elbow on March 17 and will miss the 2026 season.

Underwent an internal brace procedure on his right elbow on Oct. 29, which comes with a recovery timetable of 12-15 months. Will miss the 2026 season and was placed on the restricted list on March 25.

136.status: Swung a bat for the first time on May 29, taking 15 swings off a tee from each side of the plate. Will return to Seattle on May 30 to determine whether his recovery process will continue in Seattle or Arizona.More »

Began a second rehab assignment on May 25.
Targeting a rehab assignment in the next week.
Undergoing an MRI on May 29 to make sure everything is clear before ramping up his throwing program.
Underwent surgery to repair his right ulnar collateral ligament in January, and will be out all season.More »

Began a rehab assignment with Triple-A Sacramento on May 19.
Will take batting practice at Oracle Park this week before beginning a rehab assignment with Triple-A Sacramento.
Received a PRP injection on May 19 in San Francisco. Will be shut down from baseball activity for the next week.
Underwent a second surgery to repair an axillary artery in his upper right arm on April 17, which will sideline him for five to six months.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with Dr. Keith Meister on March 25.
Expected to miss 12-14 months after undergoing Tommy John surgery on Sept. 24, 2025.
Expected to miss the entire 2026 season after undergoing Tommy John surgery in November 2025.

138.status: Everything checked out positively after meeting with doctor on May 26. Will start plyo program and begin taking swings as soon as May 27. Imaging of the injury showed inflammation and strain, but no structural damage.

Rehab assignment transferred to Double-A Springfield on May 26. Has played in six total games between Single-A Palm Beach and Triple-A Memphis as well. Ramping up similar to a Spring Training before activation.
Urías had progressed to dry swings and his plyo program by May 25 and could start throwing program soon.

139.status: He reported soreness in back of right shoulder on April 15. Further evaluation revealed a new injury, a subscapularis strain. Had a setback while playing catch from 75 feet, so he underwent a PRP injection. "Going to be a while," manager Kevin Cash said.

Underwent surgery to repair a right elbow flexor strain on Aug. 8, with goal to return in June or July. Throwing bullpens and making progress, but no decision on when he will progress to facing hitters.

Walls was scratched from the Rays' lineup in Baltimore on May 26 and did not start May 27. Worked out at Tropicana Field pregame on May 29 and felt good, but wasn't in the lineup. Was considered available off the bench.
Felt his back "lock up" after traveling from Toronto to Tampa Bay area on May 13-14. Has faced hitters in live BP and is doing well, so should return to active roster "pretty soon," per Cash.
Felt soreness during recent series in New York and while pitching in Baltimore on May 25. Imaging revealed mild tendon inflammation, but should resume throwing around June 1.

Signed to a Major League contract on April 14. Began a rehab assignment with one-inning outings for the Rookie-level Florida Complex League Rays on May 23 and May 26. Scheduled to join Triple-A Durham on June 2.
Did not pitch all spring while dealing with a back issue that required an injection. Also underwent an appendectomy. Playing catch with the goal to start bullpen sessions on June 8.

Initially sidelined by a right shoulder injury, had his rehab assignment stalled by a sprained ankle on April 11 then shut down May 12 due to inflammation in his left shoulder. Not taking part in baseball activities, so no timeline for return.
Scratched from May 16 lineup and placed on the IL one day later. Cash said he could require 6-8 weeks of recovery after surgery. Not taking part in workouts as of May 29.

Felt his hamstring “grab” while running to first base on an infield single May 22. Did not feel better running first to third and exited the game. MRI revealed a Grade 1 strain that will sideline him for 6-8 weeks.

Underwent season-ending surgery on May 13. Multiple exams revealed "normal" wear and tear on labrum and femoroacetabular impingement, which limited his leg's range of motion. Expecting a six-month recovery followed by a full offseason to be ready for Spring Training.
Sustained a Grade 2 left ankle sprain while running the bases for Triple-A Durham on April 22. Imaging revealed no fracture.

140.status: Took live batting practice on May 29 and is expected to begin a rehab assignment with Triple-A Round Rock the following day. Expected to return at the start of the next homestand

Came out of the game on May 23 feeling fatigue and soreness in his shoulder, and woke up next day feeling it even more.
Had a setback in his throwing program, but began throwing from 60 feet during the Rangers’ most recent homestand and has been cleared to begin his progression again.
Reached 97 mph in one inning of a rehab appearance with Triple-A Round Rock on April 23 but experienced a setback and has been shut down.
Examined by team doctors on April 20 for shoulder soreness and was placed on the IL on April 23.
Signed to a Major League deal on Feb. 13, though is continuing to rehab from Tommy John surgery. Should be back on a big league mound around the Trade Deadline.

Remains in Florida. Manager John Schneider did not have an immediate update on his throwing status but mentioned he would be checking on it.

Underwent surgery to repair the labral tear and is hoping to return to the Blue Jays in five to six months. This timeline should be clearer by June, but any return in 2026 would be a success.

Playing catch out to 105 feet. Has started the throwing program portion of building back up, per interim manager Don Mattingly.
Initially felt something in his calf while doing agility work on April 14. Began a rehab assignment on May 9, and has progressed to pitching multiple innings.

An MRI revealed a strain in Farmer’s right forearm, placing him on the 10-day injured list on May 18.

Received an encouraging update when he met with doctors in early May. But it’s too early to know when he might be cleared to begin throwing. Underwent arthroscopic surgery to remove bone spurs from his elbow on Feb. 18.
Expected to miss at least eight weeks. Was called for catcher's interference when Hyeseong Kim's bat hit his glove during May 10 game at Dodger Stadium.
Could be available at some point after the All-Star break.
Will miss the 2026 season after tearing his right ACL during a collision at first base during a Grapefruit League game on March 8.
Missed 2025 while recovering from left knee surgery and he had a cleanup procedure in November. There is no timetable for his return.

Threw on May 25, but still needs to build up arm strength.
Expected to play two more rehab games with Triple-A Charlotte before the team makes a decision on his next step.
Recently underwent an appendectomy, but has resumed throwing. The procedure did delay his rehab from Tommy John surgery, though.
Had a setback in his rehab process likely pushing him into the second half of the season. His timeline is uncertain at this point.
Hicks had felt general soreness in the area for close to two weeks. But it grew increasingly worse, so the White Sox made the move.

Sprained lateral collateral ligament in his right knee on May 16 during rehab assignment with Triple-A Charlotte. Expected to require 3-6 weeks of rest and rehabilitation before he can return to play.

Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with the procedure performed by Dr. Keith Meister, but was with the team for the home opener.
Underwent successful surgery to repair the torn ACL on Oct. 28. Estimated recovery time is eight months, per the team.

According to manager Clayton McCullough, Conine is running at 70 percent, is hitting and throwing. Trending toward a quicker timeline of return. Underwent left hamstring tendon excision week of April 13.

147.status: Has continued hitting and is performing plyometric exercises. Received positive results from imaging the week of May 25 and has begun running outside.

Throwing off a mound and is scheduled to throw a live batting practice session the week of June 1, potentially June 3.

Shut down from throwing for a few weeks. Dr. Neal ElAttrache and team physician Dr. Chris Ahmad agreed on diagnosis and treatment, team spokesperson said May 16. Repeat imaging will be performed when asymptomatic.

Threw 10 pitches from a mound April 10, his first time doing so since Tommy John surgery in July 2025. Could face hitters in June and is on track for MLB activation sometime in August.

158.status: Sent to Triple-A Nashville on May 29 to begin what is expected to be a multi-outing rehab assignment, and in this case, the Brewers hope to see some velocity. (last updated: May 29)

Instead of the five innings or 80-85 pitches he was scheduled for, lasted for two outs and 38 pitches (18 strikes) in his latest rehab start for Triple-A Nashville on May 27. In six rehab starts so far, he has been charged with 24 runs on 19 hits and 17 walks in 10 innings. (last updated: May 29)
Tightened up in the middle innings of a May 22 start against the Dodgers and worsened in the ensuing days. (last updated: May 26)
Next step is scheduled to be a four "up" live batting practice session of about 60 pitches during the week of June 1. (last updated: May 26)

Slid into a concrete section of the sidewall while chasing a foul ball on May 8 against the Yankees. Resumed running during the week of May 18 and was cleared to hit off a machine on May 26. Will travel with the Brewers during their May 29-31 series in Houston. (last updated: May 26)
Currently on rehab assignment with Triple-A Nashville. (last updated: May 18)
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery in Dallas on May 11. (last updated: May 12)
